channel.title: Posts on Digital & AI Frontiers
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Digital & AI Frontiers

item.title: The Future of AI Architecture
item.description: Software architecture used to be purely deterministic. With AI models in the loop, we have to handle probabilism at the core data layer. This fundamentally changes how we write APIs, manage state, and structure databases.

item.title: AI News: The Shift in Open Source
item.description:
Several major AI labs open-sourced their model weights this week with very permissive licenses.

Why It Matters

This lowers the barrier to entry for solo developers and small teams trying to build local AI tools.

My Take & Long-term Implications

We are moving from a scarce model economy to an abundant one. The real value for startups will likely shift toward proprietary data and specialized fine-tuning.

item.title: Testing the Latest Coding Agents
item.description:
I spent the weekend testing some new coding agents. Here are my notes on how they compare.

The Setup

I asked each agent to scaffold a basic React and Postgres app.

Results

Some struggled with basic boilerplate, while others built the whole pipeline without much intervention…

item.title: My First Post
item.description:
Welcome to my new blog!

This is my very first post on this new Hugo site. I am testing out the PaperMod theme.

  • Point 1
  • Point 2
  • Point 3

Here is a quick snippet of code:

console.log("Hello, world!");

item.title: Monetizing Open Source AI Tools
item.description:
How do product companies survive when the core models are free?

The “Service-Wrapped” Model

A common pattern is turning difficult open-source deployments into 1-click services. The value is simply saving people time.

Market Observations

Startups focused on workflow integration instead of raw intelligence seem to get more B2B customers right now.

item.title: Quick Thoughts on Context Windows
item.description:
“In the age of infinite context, retrieval is still king.”

Even as LLM context windows get larger, efficient RAG is still necessary. Dumping the whole codebase into a prompt technically works, but it is slow and degrades the model’s focus. Feeding it exact snippets is much better.
